ddls:
CREATE TABLE "rx_tx_sets" (
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reception/transmission set */,
  "txrx_set_type_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the type of transmission/reception configuration */,
  "spacing1" REAL /* Spacing parameter 1 (e.g., distance between antennas) */,
  "spacing2" REAL /* Spacing parameter 2 */,
  "spacing3" REAL /* Spacing parameter 3 */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency of the transmission/reception set */,
  "modulation_technique" TEXT /* Modulation technique used (e.g., QPSK, QAM) */,
  "channel_bandwidth" REAL /* Channel bandwidth of the transmission/reception set */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the creator */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was created */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the modifier */,
  "modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rx_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rx_tx_sets_txrx_set_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("txrx_set_type_id") REFERENCES txrx_set_types ("txrx_set_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "txrx_set_types" (
  "txrx_set_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration type */,
  "standard" TEXT /* Standard or protocol associated with the configuration type */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the configuration_standards table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("txrx_set_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_txrx_set_types_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES configuration_standards ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the network site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the site */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Contact person for the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email of the contact person */,
  "site_status" TEXT /* Status of the site (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_history" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change log entry */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the reception/transmission set that changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the configuration change */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change made to the configuration */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the reception/transmission set */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the performance measurement */,
  "signal_strength" REAL /* Measured signal strength in dBm */,
  "data_throughput" REAL /* Data throughput in Mbps */,
  "interference_level" REAL /* Interference level in dBm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* User's role (e.g., engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reception/transmission set accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_status" TEXT /* Status of the access (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., low signal strength, high interference) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "site_configurations" (
  "site_config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site-configuration mapping */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where the configuration is applied */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reception/transmission set configuration */,
  "deployment_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the configuration was deployed */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the configuration was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_configurations_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_configurations_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES network_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the standard or protocol (e.g., 3GPP, IEEE) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the standard */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the standard (e.g., wireless, wired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency record */,
  "dependent_rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dependent reception/transmission set */,
  "required_rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the required reception/transmission set */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., hard, soft) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_dependencies_dependent_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_dependencies_required_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("required_rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reception/transmission set the comment refers to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating of the comment (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_comments_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "integrated_systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reception/transmission set */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the integrated system */,
  "system_type" TEXT /* Type of the integrated system (e.g., network monitoring, analytics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_integrated_systems_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "rx_set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reception/transmission set */,
  "permission_type" TEXT /* Type of permission (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_rx_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("rx_set_id") REFERENCES rx_tx_sets ("rx_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
